content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY NOVEMBER 2004 37th UPDATE – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY NOVEMBER 2004 37th UPDATE Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY NOVEMBER 2004 37th UPDATE Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY NOVEMBER 2004 37th UPDATE Introduction The 37 th Update of the Civil Procedure Rules introduce a number of notable changes to procedures, including the following; A new Section II of Part 41 is inserted, making provision about the exercise of the court’s powers under section 2(1) of the Damages Act 1996 to order that all or part of an award of damages in respect of personal injury is to take the form of periodical payments. Amendments have been made to Part 36 to ensure that the scheme for offers to settle and payments into court can work in cases in which periodical payments may be awarded. The Practice Direction supplementing Part 49 (Applications under the Companies Act etc.) is amended to make provision for two types of application. New paragraph 3B deals with applications relating to the formation of a European public limited-liability company (or Societas Europaea) by merger under Council Regulation (EC) No 2157/2001 of 8 October 2001 on the Statute for a European company (SE). New paragraph 14 deals with applications under section 59 of the Criminal Justice and Police Act 2001 relating to property seized in connection with a company investigation under Part XIV of the Companies Act 1985. The Practice Direction supplementing Part 52 is amended by adding a new paragraph 23.8C dealing with appeals to the High Court against a decision of the Secretary of State or a national financial supervisory authority to oppose certain steps being taken in relation to a European public limited-liability company. A full list of the changes is set out below; Rules Part 36 In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.3 , cross-reference inserted to take into account new rule 36.2A. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK New rule 36.2A inserted. In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.4 , cross-references inserted to take into account new rule 36.2A. In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.10 , new paragraph inserted (3A), and cross-references inserted to take account of new paragraph (3A). In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.20 , new paragraph inserted (1(c). In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.21 , cross-reference inserted to take into account new rule 36.2A. In THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK rule 36.23(4) , reference to rule 36.20 amended to 36.20(1)(a), and THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK new paragraph added (4A). Part 41 Table of contents amended to take into account new Section II. In rule 41.1 , cross-reference inserted to take into account new Section II. New Section II inserted after rule 41.3. Practice Directions Practice Direction 21 In paragraph 6.1, heading amended and reference to paragraph 6.4 added to sub-paragraph (3). In paragraph 6.3, new sub-paragraph (3) inserted. Paragraph 6.4 replaced. After paragraph 6.5, new paragraphs 6.6–6.9 inserted. Practice Direction 29 Heading to paragraph 3A replaced with new text. Paragraph 3A replaced with new text. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 36 In paragraph 1.7 cross-reference inserted to refer to new rule 36.2A. New paragraph 5.3A inserted after paragraph 5.3. New paragraph 7.2A inserted after paragraph 7.2. In paragraph 7.11, cross-reference inserted to refer to new rule 36.2A. New paragraph 12 inserted. Practice Direction 40C Is revoked. Practice Direction 41B New practice direction inserted to deal with new periodical payments provisions of Part 41 – “Periodical Payments Under the Damages Act 1996”. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 49B The title of the Practice Direction is amended. In paragraph 1, new definitions of “the CJPA,” “the EC Regulation” and “SE” are inserted. In paragraph 2(1), references to the EC Regulation and CJPA are inserted. Paragraph 3 is amended to refer to all applications to which the Practice Direction applies. After paragraph 3A, a new paragraph 3B is inserted, setting out the procedures for applications relating to the formation of a European public limited-liability company. Paragraph 10 is amended to refer to all applications to which the Practice Direction applies. After paragraph 13, a new paragraph 14 is inserted, setting out the procedure for applications relating to property seized in connection with a company investigation. Practice Direction 52 Paragraph 13.2 replaced with new text. Paragraph 13.5 replaced with new text. In paragraph 23.2, a new sub-paragraph (18) is inserted. After paragraph 23.8A, a new paragraph 23.8B is inserted, setting out the procedure for appeals against the opposition of the Secretary of State or a national financial supervisory authority to certain steps being taken in relation to a European public limited-liability company.
